reviews.review_description: This is great a tool to visit a place that I may never visit in real life. Thanks to the effort and dedication of the creators for this project I really feel closer to visiting such amazing places. Great job! 👏
I have experienced environments I didn't know existed. And I can visit again and again. I wish more countries would do what you have so exquisitely done. Thank you.
I’ve been 3 times and this definitely helps with the blues on not returning this year.

I’ve tried several travel apps over the years I’ve had a Quest headset, and this is by far the best. The scenery is breathtaking, and there are lots of different places to visit. I especially love all the ones with water—I can just sit there and stare at the water, listening to the soothing sounds of waves or waterfalls, and just unwind. I tried the 15 minute sample and then immediately bought the full version. Well worth it!

Unreal. Another masterpiece from the developer who brought us Teleport Scotland.

I’ve always dreamed of going to Iceland, but the cost was always out of reach. This app gave me that experience in the most breathtaking way. It’s hauntingly beautiful—so immersive that it actually brought me to tears.

The historical perspective added so much depth, but it was also bittersweet. Knowing that climate change is rapidly destroying glaciers, and that many of these landscapes may not exist in the future, broke my heart. Being able to stand in these places virtually, without harming the environment, is something truly special.

I also want to acknowledge the sheer effort it must have taken to capture these vantage points. Many looked slippery, treacherous, and incredibly hard to reach—especially while hauling equipment. That kind of dedication is inspiring, and I deeply respect it.

Please keep making these experiences. They’re not just apps; they’re portals to the beauty of our world that everyone deserves to see.

Very high quality 360° VR loops, well put together for a reasonable price (less than the price of one Icelandic coffee after postage and packing).
The locations are great and it includes a guide telling you a bit about the place. There's a map to navigate or you can grip-trigger to a random spot. It has an overall polished look and feel - this hasn't just been thrown together one afternoon.
Slight fault on one of the South coast waterfall scenes.
Few ideas to improve : a clearer picture on the map of visited places or some resumable guided itinery for a more A - Z experience. Greater control and visual marking of going back and forth not just a button. Did you include the famous Icelandic magic museum - we want to see the necropants!
Good piece of atmospheric software giving a solid feel for the place. Skál!
This is a proper vr travel experience. Will be spending a lot more time with this one.

I already loved Teleport Scotland, but Iceland is magic. I already wanted to visit Iceland, but now even more. Stunning locations in high quality. I really hope there is more to come in the Teleport series.

reviews.review_title: Best App I’ve purchased to date
reviews.review_description: I’ve been to Iceland and this app makes me feel like I’m there again. The visuals are amazing, well worth the price.
Great app, but hasn't had any updates since it came out over a year ago.  Locations are old now  and not woth a resist.  New content as promised please.

Beautifully done 360 video of amazing locations and different points of view in those locations. Will gladly pay for anything and everything this creator does in the future. This is a no-brainer purchase for anyone wanting quality travel experiences.

3 star Michelin chef's kiss to the developer. Mind blowing. Stunning. VR minvacation. 16K resolution or looks like it. Fantastic. Epic. Voyage. Simply can not wait until Teleport Scotland VR from the same developer is released on meta quest. I'd scoop that one up for sure especially if it does that winding curving famous walkway in Edinburgh.
The nature scenes remain more or less unchanged for centuries. When you get to a city like for eg Akureyri, it is a stale static snapshot in time. A few years later, you'll still see the same guy wearing the orange puffy jacket, etc A live cam VR set up that does a new vignette once every few months would be an interesting idea. VR is a new horizon. Get in on the ground floor. So many ideas for VR aps. An app already does that. It's called Wander. Unlike with Earthwquest, Wander lets you time travel to the past. It's updated about once every few years consistent with Google map street view.

This is the best travel/exploration app currently out there. I enjoy Wander and Brink too - but I like the idea of focusing on one location in detail. I tried 3 single-location in-depth travel apps and this one beat them all by a longshot - it's beautifully done, the videos are clear and gorgeous, the UX is intuitive. I want more from this dev, please.

Since I have visited Iceland, I am searching for everything related to it. Yeah, this is it. When the app has put me into Vík, I was shouting loudly: 
"Oh my God, I was standing right here when I was there! "

All the scenes are beautiful.
Giga-thanks to the debelopers.
Really enjoyed Scotland and waited a long time for a new location and Iceland is just as awesome. Thank you. Will definitely purchase any new content in the future 😊

What a wonderful experience this is. 

You'll find points of interest all across the Iceland map, and you're able to explore things local to those POIs in wonderful ways.

Some superb 360 VR photography and tidbits of info about each of the POIs make this both a great experience and something for potential Iceland tourists to have before visiting or for someone who has already visited to rekindle the joy of the experience.

5 stars.

This app absolutely amazes me! I love the fact that I can use it without the controller my eyes control it. The graphics are outstanding, probably the best I’ve seen. Sizing is superior, and by that i mean proportionally. I feel like i fit into the scene, not looking down or being among giants like so many others tried. 

Love the voice over telling me about the site. Cannot wait for Greenland to be added as my late father was stationed there during Korean war and with this i will feel like he is walking with me. 

Cannot wait for more locations to be added. Definitely worth the money.

I especially loved being so close to the waterfalls💜💜

I like the information (written or audio) tied to the locations, the navigation methods that don't require controllers, and the constant loop video that makes it feel more like you're actually at the place compared to a still 360 environment. Looking forward to more Teleport content.

reviews.review_title: I have always wanted to visit Iceland.
reviews.review_description:
I have always wanted to visit Iceland. Now I NEED to visit Iceland.
Immerse yourself in Iceland's breathtaking beauty with this VR app's mesmerizing visuals and insightful voiceovers. The carefully curated vignettes showcase Iceland's stunning landscapes, evoking a strong desire to experience them firsthand. With a sleek user interface, this app offers an immersive virtual journey through one of the world's most captivating destinations.

As someone who's been to Iceland and stood in many of the locations featured in the app, it's great to be able to revisit and relive some of the memories I have with this incredible place.  Beautiful selection of 360 videos in stereo from all over the island - this app makes a great primer for places to visit if you're ever planning a trip.

Nice locations, natural sounds and controller free experience. The resolution could be better but the tech isnt quite there yet. One thing that can be improved upon- I cant go back to the first or 2nd spot when at the last location, would be a good addition to be able to go backwards.

developer_response.body: Hi RoaminRound, thanks for your review, you can go back to the previous spots with the controller. You can activate the controllers via the map interface. Hope this helps.

reviews.review_description: … you have to get it! Fair price, great atmosphere. Can’t wait for „Teleport Earth“!

The scenery is overwhelmingly awesome. Nice to teleport without controllers or hands. Couldn't figure out how to go back to other teleport spots inside each scene, but maybe I missed something. Can't wait to see more locations and more features, like changing the music or turning it on/off. Great job!!!

developer_response.body: Hi LDBlaq, thanks for your review. In the map interface, there are settings for music, voice narrations and activating the controllers. With controllers, it's possible to return to the previous spots inside each scene. Hope this helps.
